english: to melt / to thaw / to dissolve
notes: Schmelzen is used both intransitively (something melts on its own, Perfekt with 'ist') and transitively (someone melts something, Perfekt with 'hat'). It is a strong verb with vowel change: schmelzen → schmilzt → schmolz → geschmolzen.

sentences.german: Im Frühling schmilzt der Schnee in der Sonne.
sentences.english: In spring, the snow melts in the sun.

sentences.german: Die Butter ist in der heißen Pfanne schnell geschmolzen.
sentences.english: The butter melted quickly in the hot pan.

sentences.german: Der Koch hat die Schokolade im Wasserbad geschmolzen.
sentences.english: The cook melted the chocolate in a double boiler.
